concept: Specification / Explicit Set of Technical Requirements
description: An explicit set of technical requirements. A specification states what is required; the exact values, wording, and version belong in the source record, and meeting the requirements says nothing yet about whether they were wise ones.
sound_symbolism: Crisp 'ki' places one bright point before the rest of the word unfolds. Stressed 'sho' spreads a soft hush into round 'o' and widens that point into a field. 'Lu' releases the tongue, lets the field continue in deep 'u', and joins exact contact, broad quiet, and liquid continuation in a set with both detail and extent.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha kisholu ha henora thelima.' (This specification describes the capacity.) Preserve exact values, units, identifiers, versions, standards, and quoted requirements in a separate source record. A system satisfies a specification with ordinary 'heno'; this does not itself certify compliance. Distinct from 'kirema' (criterion), 'norae' (method), and 'kirothe' (define).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Open specifications can make shared infrastructure understandable and repairable while leaving authorship, authority, access, and consequences open to challenge.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Separating a written requirement from authority and compliance prevents technical language from turning an instruction into unquestionable command.

tags.cognition: a reference against which properties and performance may be assessed
tags.creation: an explicit set of requirements for design, work, or technical provision
tags.dialogue: a shareable statement of requirements that may still be disputed or revised
